response:
To ensure the button area is at least 10 square inches for screen widths between 3 and 6 inches we formulate the inequality 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 >= 10 which simplifies to 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 17 >= 0. Evaluating the polynomial at the endpoints shows that at x=3 the value is 2(27) - 5(9) + 3(3) - 17 = 54 - 45 + 9 - 17 = 1 which is not greater than or equal to zero. At x=4 the value is 2(64) - 5(16) + 3(4) - 17 = 128 - 80 + 12 - 17 = 43 which satisfies the condition. Since the function is increasing in this interval the valid range for x where the area is at least 10 is approximately x >= 3.15 within the given domain of 3 to 6.
******
To find the exact screen width where the button area equals 15 square inches we solve the equation 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 = 15 which rearranges to 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 22 = 0. Testing integer values reveals that x=3 yields 2(27) - 5(9) + 3(3) - 22 = 54 - 45 + 9 - 22 = -4 while x=4 yields 2(64) - 5(16) + 3(4) - 22 = 128 - 80 + 12 - 22 = 38. The real root lies between 3 and 4 and can be approximated numerically as x ≈ 3.36 inches. A second response to this specific query involves noting that the cubic equation has only one real solution in the positive domain relevant to screen widths since the derivative 6x^2 - 10x + 3 has roots that do not create multiple local extrema in the positive region that would intersect the line y=15 more than once.
